## [00:00 - 02:58]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about the buyer's mindset. When I say that, I mean the mindset that the person has to be in in order to be ready to buy when you offer your product to them. So here's what happens. Most people never think about what a potential buyer needs to believe before they're ready to buy. But thinking through what a non buyer believes, I'm going to call that state A. And what they need to believe in order to be able to buy from you, we'll call that state B, is incredibly powerful. Because what that does is it helps you to be able to think about what you can do to get them from state A to state B. And once you start thinking about that, then all kinds of profitable options become open to you. So there are four key things that a person needs to be able to believe for key areas around which a person needs to be able to believe in order to buy from you. The first is the situation. They need to believe that the situation that you're describing, perhaps the problem, perhaps the environment, whatever it is that you're describing is real, and that there is a way to resolve that particular solution situation in their life so that their life can be better as a result. Secondly, they need to believe that the product that you're offering them is the only or the best way to resolve that current situation. Third, they need to believe something about themselves. They need to believe that the transformation is possible for them to achieve in their own life. Sometimes I find that people, they believe that it'll work for other people, but oh, this could never work for me. And then finally, they need to believe some things about you. They need to believe that you are the only or the best person to help them. So let's go through each of these four belief areas and talk about the specific things about what non-buyers believe versus what buyers believe about that particular aspect. So the buyer's belief about the situation. So state A, what do the non-buyers believe? State B, what do the buyers believe? So state A, about the situation. They need to believe, non-buyers believe that this situation can't be resolved. Now, by the way, these are not necessarily all true.

## [03:00 - 06:05]

Some of them will be true in almost every situation. So it could be that your non-buyers believe that this situation just can't be resolved, that this is just the way it is, or maybe that it's going to be too hard, or maybe impossible to resolve, or that the situation will probably improve on its own over time, or that it's not urgent to solve that problem. But buyers believe that the problem is real, that an alternative actually exists to that particular problem, and that that problem is hurting their sales, or their business, or their life, or their self-esteem, or their relationship, or whatever that is, that it is in your area that you're talking about. And they also, it helps that they believe that it's going to be hard to find a good solution, so they'll be willing to buy yours, and that it may not improve, or it may get worse, unless they find a solution themselves, and that it is in their business, or their lifestyle, or reputation, whatever is at stake, and that they really need to get this thing solved as soon as possible. So let me just explain what we're doing here. What non-buyers believe, the ideal is to shift that set of mindsets that's on the left side into the set of mindsets that are on the right side, because the right side is where you're preparing them to be willing to hand you money to solve these problems for them. All right, so now let's talk about the product, and what non-buyers believe versus buyers. So once you start talking about your product, non-buyers believe that, hey, this product isn't worth the investment that you're asking for from them, and that investment is both monetary, as well as time, and sacrifice, and all the non-monetary things that go with that particular product. They may believe that the solution you're offering them is old or out of date, or that this particular product won't apply to them, or that they can find that information free, or paid maybe from someone else, or paid for a cheaper amount, or whatever, elsewhere, or that maybe your product won't actually give them the transformation that they're after. Now, when you shift to a set of beliefs like these that I'm going to show you in just a second, then they tend to move more towards being buyers. So the buyers believe that your product is the best or the only way to solve this problem, or that your product is actually going to work, or that it's manageable, that it's doable, or that it will give them new insight into the situation,

## [06:05 - 09:12]

that it's current, that it's not dated, that it's the best path to solving their problem, and that this product that you're going to offer to them is worth the investment, both monetary and non-monetary, and that, hey, other people have achieved that desired result, and so they're likely to do the same thing too. Now, what do they need to believe about themselves? So non-buyers believe that they're too busy, they don't have enough money, they're different, the transformation won't work for them, that's actually pretty key, because they may say I'm too old, I'm too fat, I'm too set in my ways, whatever that is, they tend to believe that, hey, I'm an exception, this won't work for me, that's one reason why it's so advantageous to have a number of testimonials, because then they can look at it and say, oh, that person's like me, maybe all those other ones aren't, but that person's like me, or they believe that they can just do it alone, so buyers believe that the promise transformation actually applies to them, that it won't work for me, obstacle is overcome, because it will, they believe it actually will work for them, they believe that they really need it, and that they have the time to do it, and both to learn and to implement the product, they believe that they have what it takes mentally and emotionally to make that transformation, and that they need outside help, that they can't do it alone, or that it will be so much more efficient if they don't try to do it alone, that they get your help, and that they are at risk of further suffering if they don't get help. And finally, there's some things they need to believe about you. Non-buyers believe that you can't be trusted, that you haven't proven that you can provide the results, that you aren't the expert, or the expert that's right for them, that you're just after their money. Buyers believe that you're the real deal, that you have a proven method, or a system, or a process, that you're trustworthy, that you're the expert that's the right person to work with them, and that you're capable and reliable, and that you really have a true desire to serve them, and see them succeed. So there's a lot of things here, we actually have a really good, we have a handout that shows you all of these, and that asks you to go through and think about how in each of these areas can you move people, what can you say, do, demonstrate, show, provide testimonials about, whatever those things are, what can you do to move people from this non-buyers belief state, and actually shift their mindsets when they're now believing the things that are in the buyer's mindset. The reason we're doing this is because those things that we're talking about
